headline: Transcript: Trump’s Russian oil sanctions shake energy markets
summary: Marc Filippino talks to Jamie Smyth and Ciara Nugent
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Trump’s Russian oil sanctions shake energy markets’

Marc FilippinoGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Friday, October 24th, and this is your FT News Briefing. Sanctions on Russian oil groups have thrown a wrench into the oil market. And the US pardoned another crypto executive yesterday. Plus, Argentina heads to the polls for midterm elections this weekend. It’s a make-or-break moment for President Javier Milei.

Ciara NugentIf Milei loses the midterms, Trump could pull the plug on that loan, which would be pretty disastrous at this point for the peso.

Marc FilippinoI’m Marc Filippino, and here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

US President Donald Trump this week imposed what he described as massive sanctions on Russian oil giants, Rosneft and Lukoil, and their subsidiaries.

Karoline Leavitt voice clipLook, the president has always maintained that he would implement sanctions on
